description: secretx-k8s/src/lib.rs line 123 has bs.0.clone() where bs comes from data.get(k.as_str()). Because data is a local BTreeMap that is consumed later in the function anyway, the borrow can be dissolved by using data.remove(k.as_str()).map(|bs| bs.0) instead, which takes ownership of the ByteString value and avoids the heap allocation. The clone is not required for correctness — it exists only to satisfy the borrow checker while keeping the shared reference to data. Switching to remove() eliminates the unnecessary allocation and makes the intent explicit.
title: gratuitous clone() to escape borrow checker in secretx-k8s get()
